notable_text: Face the Consequences; 18 U.S.C.; Sec.111 Assaulting/Impeding/Obstructing up to 20 years + $250K; Sec.2261A Cyberstalking up to 5 years; Sec.875 Online Threatening up to 20 years; Sec.119 Doxing up to 5 years
description: A square graphic in the ICE color palette. The top is a red banner with white text in mixed serif and italic styling reading 'Face the Consequences' ('the' in italic). The main body has a solid blue background with a faint ICE/DHS seal watermark behind the text. A large white serif headline reads '18 U.S.C.' (United States Code). Below it is a list of four federal criminal statutes, each formatted as a section symbol and number in serif beside a bold uppercase white offense label and red-highlighted penalty: 'Sec. 111 ASSAULTING, IMPEDING OR OBSTRUCTING UP TO 20 YEARS IN PRISON + $250K IN FINES'; 'Sec. 2261A CYBERSTALKING UP TO 5 YEARS IN PRISON'; 'Sec. 875 ONLINE THREATENING UP TO 20 YEARS IN PRISON'; 'Sec. 119 DOXING UP TO 5 YEARS IN PRISON'. At the bottom center is the white DHS/ICE seal with the 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: FOX NEWS; CALIFORNIA; ICE arrests 100+ illegal alien truckers in major sweep after deadly crashes across multiple states; POLICE U.S. CUSTOMS AND BORDER PROTECTION; USCG
description: A vertical share-card graphic styled like a Fox News social-media post. The top has a torn-paper white band with the red, white and blue 'FOX NEWS channel' logo centered. Below is a small red 'CALIFORNIA' tag, then a bold black headline: 'ICE arrests 100+ illegal alien truckers in major sweep after deadly crashes across multiple states'. The lower portion is a full-width photograph of a group of men, faces blurred, lined up and being processed by officers wearing tactical gear and vests labeled 'POLICE / U.S. CUSTOMS AND BORDER PROTECTION' and yellow 'USCG' vests. Inset at lower right is a smaller bordered photo of officers and an inspector in an orange hi-vis vest beside a red semi-truck cab numbered 8205 and a shipping container.

notable_text: Over 5,300 criminal aliens arrested throughout L.A., including his district.; U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement
description: Vertical graphic built around a desaturated blue-toned photograph of Congressman Robert Garcia, a clean-shaven man in a dark suit and open-collar white shirt with a small U.S. flag lapel pin, shown mid-speech with mouth open and one hand raised, against a dark background. The photo is tinted in a blue monochrome. Overlaid on the right side in large type is the word 'Over' in gold italic, then '5,300' in large bold gold numerals, followed by 'criminal aliens arrested throughout L.A., including his district.' in white serif. A navy-blue footer bar runs across the bottom containing the circular DHS seal next to white text 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.'

genre_refs: FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitive poster format
notable_text: FBI TEN MOST WANTED FUGITIVE; EDUARDO RAVELO; REWARD up to $100,000
description: Color graphic of an FBI 'TEN MOST WANTED FUGITIVE' poster. The layout uses the FBI template: a white background with a deep-blue masthead bar reading 'FBI TEN MOST WANTED FUGITIVE' in bold serif condensed caps, with the gold and blue FBI / Department of Justice seal at top left. Below the masthead, the name 'EDUARDO RAVELO' appears in large blue caps. A paragraph of blue text lists the federal charges: 'Conspiracy to Conduct the Affairs of an Enterprise, Through a Pattern of Racketeering Activity; Conspiracy to Distribute and Possess with the Intent to Distribute Controlled Substances; Conspiracy to Import Controlled Substances; Conspiracy to Launder Monetary Instruments; Conspiracy to Kill Persons in a Foreign Country; Murder Resulting from the Use and Carrying of a Firearm During and in Relation to Crimes of Violence and Drug Trafficking; Murder in Aid of Racketeering Activity.' Beneath the charges are four images in a row: a 1998 mugshot photograph of a Hispanic man with a goatee, a pencil 'Composite Sketch,' and two 1992 photographs showing tattoos on the man's chest and abdomen and on his back (each captioned with the year taken). A 'DESCRIPTION' header introduces a two-column biographical block listing Aliases ('Tablas,' 'T-Blas,' '2x4,' 'Blas,' 'Lumberman,' 'Boards,' 'Tablero,' '56,' etc.), Dates of Birth Used, Place of Birth (Mexico), Hair, Eyes, Height (5'9"), Weight, Build, Complexion, Sex, Race ('White (Hispanic)'), Nationality (Mexican), and Scars and Marks. A red 'REWARD' bar states the FBI is offering up to $100,000 for information leading to his arrest.

notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday - the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
description: A slogan graphic built around a stock photograph: two people embracing in a hug, in what appears to be an airport terminal (blurred travelers and concourse lighting in the background). One person wears a denim jacket and a small backpack and has a face mask pulled down; the other wears a light-colored sweater. Overlaid white text in the lower-left reads 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday' (with 'MAY' in larger type), followed by a large headline 'the LEGAL way' where the word 'LEGAL' is in bold green and 'the' and 'way' are white. Across the bottom is a dark blue banner bearing the round DHS/ICE seal and the 'ICE' wordmark on the left, with a vertical divider and the line 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation' on the right.

notable_text: JD VANCE: NATIVE-BORN CITIZENS ARE DRIVING GROWTH - 'JOBS ARE GOING TO AMERICAN WORKERS FOR A CHANGE'; AMERICAN JOBS!
description: A card built from a screenshot of a Breitbart News article. The top has a large bold black all-caps headline 'JD VANCE: NATIVE-BORN CITIZENS ARE DRIVING GROWTH, JOBS ARE GOING TO AMERICAN WORKERS FOR A CHANGE'. Beneath the headline is a row of social-share buttons: a blue Facebook button showing '64', a black 'X / TWEET' button, a gray 'EMAIL' button, and a blue 'TRUTH' button. Below that, framed by a thick orange border, is a video still: JD Vance, in a dark suit, white shirt and red tie, with a beard, seated and speaking, an American flag to his left and faded 'BREITBART' text behind him; a white play-button triangle is over the center and a white Breitbart 'B' logo is in the upper right. Across the bottom of the still in large bold white block letters with a drop shadow is the phrase 'AMERICAN JOBS!'.

notable_text: THE DAILY WIRE; EXCLUSIVE: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services Will Only Recognize Two Genders; The decision will impact green cards and employment authorization documents.
description: A clean, designed graphic styled as a screenshot/share card of a Daily Wire article header. White background. At top center is the masthead 'THE DAILY WIRE' in the publication's distinctive thin-serif caps with horizontal rules flanking the small word 'THE'. A thin grey divider line runs beneath. Below at left a red eyebrow/kicker reads 'EXCLUSIVE' in spaced caps. The main headline in large black sans-serif reads: 'EXCLUSIVE: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services Will Only Recognize Two Genders'. A bold black sub-headline/dek follows: 'The decision will impact green cards and employment authorization documents.' There are no photos, seals, or illustrations; the content is text only.

notable_text: Break our laws, lose your privilege.; PERMANENT RESIDENT; IMMIGRANT VISA; SPECIMEN / TEST V; US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON
description: A slogan graphic on a light blue gradient background faintly watermarked with the circular seal text 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY'. The headline at top, in large dark-navy type, reads 'Break our laws,' on the first line (bold) and 'lose your privilege.' on the second (italic). Centered below are images of two immigration documents shown as specimens: the upper card is a 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA PERMANENT RESIDENT' green card (surname 'SPECIMEN', given name 'TEST V', USCIS# 000-000-310, Category IR1, with a Statue of Liberty and flag design and a sample photo), and the lower card is an 'IMMIGRANT VISA' issued by the 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON' (surname 'TRAVELER', IV Case Number LND2004I6000201, Registration Number 00000473). Both documents are blurred and pixelated over the personal-data fields. The color scheme uses the DHS palette and seal.

notable_text: (no headline slogan; cartel insignia stamps on drug bricks; 'BLACK AND WHITE' label on a bundle; mugshot height-scale numbers 60-69)
description: Designed composite card combining drug-evidence imagery with an inset booking photograph. The main background photograph shows many kilo-sized bricks individually wrapped in clear plastic (some stamped with a circular insignia), stacked in a pyramid on a wooden surface against a plain wall, with additional taped bundles and bags of white powder in the foreground (one bundle showing 'BLACK AND WHITE' lettering). Inset in the upper-right corner, framed as a separate panel, is a booking photograph of a bald, older man in a white T-shirt photographed against a height-measurement backdrop (numbers '69 / 66 / 63 / 60' visible on the side scale).

notable_text: American Jobs for AMERICAN WORKERS; The WHITE HOUSE; all job gains have gone to native-born Americans; more than 2.5 million native-born Americans gained employment, while 670,000 foreign-born workers lost employment
description: A statistics card on a dark navy background. The top half is a photograph of President Trump seated at the Resolute Desk in the Oval Office (dark suit, white shirt, dark red tie, lapel pins), with the leather chair, framed family photos and a U.S. Navy flag behind him; he is looking off to the side. Across the lower-middle in large serif type is the text 'American Jobs for' (white italic) above 'AMERICAN WORKERS' (bold white caps). Below sits a small dark badge reading 'The WHITE HOUSE'. Underneath, inside a white text box, centered serif body text states: 'Under President Trump, all job gains have gone to native-born Americans, reversing the Biden-era trend. Over the past year, more than 2.5 million native-born Americans gained employment, while 670,000 foreign-born workers lost employment.' A small circular White House seal icon is centered at the bottom.

notable_text: (no designed headline; subject wears a Philadelphia 'EAGLES' t-shirt)
description: A composite card built around an inset close-up portrait of a man (identified in the tweet as alleged MS-13 gang member Carlos Rivera-Alvarado of El Salvador). The inset is a tightly cropped frontal photo of a man with short dark hair and light stubble, wearing a dark gray-green Philadelphia Eagles t-shirt (the team's bird logo and 'EAGLES' wordmark are visible), shot against a plain pale interior wall. This portrait is layered over a background photo of a residential and commercial street scene: a low pale building, a wide empty street, sidewalk, parked cars, telephone and utility poles and lines, and trees under a clear blue sky. The tweet states the location is Vineland, N.J. No agency seal or on-image headline text is present in this version; the only text is the 'EAGLES' branding on the subject's shirt.

notable_text: FOX NEWS / SANCTUARY CITIES / ICE arrests Latin Kings member after NYC sanctuary release despite assault charge on first responder / DHS says Bryan David Tasiguano Leon, an Ecuadorian national, was arrested by ICE on March 4 after the NYPD freed him over federal objection / POLICE ICE
description: A card styled as a torn-paper / newspaper collage built around a Fox News story. The upper portion is a light off-white 'newsprint' panel bearing the FOX NEWS logo, a small red 'SANCTUARY CITIES' tag, and a bold black headline: 'ICE arrests Latin Kings member after NYC sanctuary release despite assault charge on first responder,' with a gray subhead 'DHS says Bryan David Tasiguano Leon, an Ecuadorian national, was arrested by ICE on March 4 after the NYPD freed him over federal objection.' A torn edge separates this from the lower photographic half, which shows the torso of an ICE officer in tactical gear: a gray plate-carrier vest with a large yellow 'POLICE / ICE' patch and dangling flex-cuffs, with other agents partly visible. Inset at lower-right is a rectangular booking photo of a man with short dark hair wearing a white hoodie, looking at the camera.

notable_text: COMMACK (printed on the subject's sweatshirt)
description: Composite graphic. A booking-style photo inset (with rounded corners and a thin border) is layered over a faded full-bleed background photograph of a suburban residential street with trees, fences, utility poles and houses under a partly cloudy blue sky. The inset photo shows a young man with dark, voluminous curly hair, a thin mustache and soul patch, looking at the camera against a plain light-gray wall; he wears a dark hooded sweatshirt printed with bowling-pin graphics and the word 'COMMACK.' There is no large headline on this image.

notable_text: America First. Americans First.
description: A slogan graphic with a warm, film-grain aesthetic. The photograph shows a large American flag waving on a pole at center, backlit by a soft pink-and-lavender dusk sky, with a range of blue mountains catching golden sunset light along the lower portion of the frame. A grain/vignette gives it a vintage 'analog film' look. Centered across the top in white serif typography is the two-line slogan 'America First.' (upright) and 'Americans First.' (italic), with a tiny faint circular DOL/government seal watermark just above. There is no other text. The tweet text refers to employment gains to American-born workers and cracking down on H-1B 'abuse'.

A composite graphic: a booking/mugshot photo inset is layered over a faded, blue-tinted full-bleed background photograph of a sunny suburban residential street lined with small single-story houses, parked cars (including a Jeep), trees and utility poles under a clear blue sky. The centered inset photo shows a middle-aged man with short dark hair and a full dark beard, wearing a light gray/sage hooded sweatshirt, standing against an interior office wall with a drop-ceiling, doors and a numbered placard ('4') behind him, looking at the camera. No large headline text appears on this image. The tweet states the subject is Magdaleno Roman-Rosales of Mexico, previously convicted of unlawful restraint/kidnapping, removed, and who reentered illegally before being re-arrested.

notable_text: DAILY SOUTHWEST BORDER APPREHENSIONS / AVERAGE PER DAY / 230 NOW / 5,100 UNDER BIDEN ADMIN / CBP: MARCH NUMBERS SHOW MOST SECURE BORDER IN HISTORY
description: Still styled as a Fox Business cable-news broadcast graphic. The background is a photograph of the U.S.-Mexico border wall (tall rust-colored steel bollard fencing) crossing desert scrub terrain. A bold black banner across the top reads 'DAILY SOUTHWEST BORDER APPREHENSIONS' with a yellow sub-banner 'AVERAGE PER DAY.' Below are two large side-by-side blue stat panels: '230 NOW' and '5,100 UNDER BIDEN ADMIN' (white numerals). Small text reads 'U.S. CUSTOMS AND BORDER PROTECTION.' A Fox Business chyron at the bottom reads 'CBP: MARCH NUMBERS SHOW MOST SECURE BORDER IN HISTORY', alongside ticker elements: a 'THE HOT TOPIC' bug, 'FOX BUSINESS' logo, time '4:01A PT', 'DOW FUTURES 42,038.00 / -203.00', and 'CRUDE OIL 70.77 / -0.43', plus currency tickers (AUD/USD, KRW/USD).

genre_refs: App Store / Google Play app-promo ad
notable_text: CBP Home App; El portal unico que agiliza muchos servicios de la CBP; Intencion de Salir; Welcome To CBP Home; LOGIN OR SIGN UP
description: Horizontal app-promotion graphic for the CBP Home mobile app, in Spanish, in navy blue, gold/yellow, white, and red. At left is a smartphone mockup showing the app's welcome screen: a US flag and 'Official App of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security' header, the DHS seal, an illustrated officer icon, the heading 'Welcome To CBP Home', subtext 'A single portal to multiple CBP services to streamline your experience', and a red 'LOGIN OR SIGN UP' button with 'with LOGIN.GOV'. The background is a tinted blue photo of hands holding a phone. Large white bold display text reads 'CBP Home App' with a gold subtitle bar 'El portal unico que agiliza muchos servicios de la CBP'. A body paragraph in Spanish describes the 'Intencion de Salir' (Intent to Depart) feature, stating it offers aliens who are illegally in the country, or whose parole has been revoked, a voluntary, orderly, defined process to notify the government of their intention to leave the United States. The 'U.S. Customs and Border Protection' seal/wordmark sits at lower right, with App Store and Google Play download badges at top right. The tweet states that those who self-deport via the app receive $1,000 upon confirmed return home.

notable_text: THIS IS ONE OF THE MOST EGREGIOUS CRIMINAL OFFENSES THAT I'VE COME ACROSS IN MY ENTIRE CAREER. - GABRIEL MARTINEZ
description: Vertical quote card graphic built on a tightly cropped, low light close-up photograph of a man's face, identified in the tweet as Honduran national Jose Ignacio Bonilla-Garcia, in portrait style with shadow across the face. The upper portion overlays a bold all caps white sans-serif quote: 'THIS IS ONE OF THE MOST EGREGIOUS CRIMINAL OFFENSES THAT I'VE COME ACROSS IN MY ENTIRE CAREER.' attributed to 'GABRIEL MARTINEZ' (with a smaller title line beneath, an ICE/ERO official). The image is dark and high contrast. The tweet describes an alleged beating and rape behind a dumpster in New York.

notable_text: Putting Americans FIRST!; The Dallas Express; secretary-certified investigations that can proceed without a formal worker complaint... reasonable cause to believe an employer may be breaking the law.
description: Vertical graphic combining a photograph of Donald Trump with a press excerpt. The top two-thirds is a photograph of Trump from the chest up, wearing a navy suit, white shirt, red tie, and an American-flag lapel pin, looking slightly upward and off-camera against a blurred crowd background. Across the lower third, on a dark navy panel, a white italic and bold serif headline reads 'Putting Americans FIRST!'. Below it sits a white rounded text box containing the masthead of 'The Dallas Express' (blackletter logo) followed by a quoted passage: 'Under President Donald Trump, the Labor Department has begun using an authority that lawyers reportedly said had gone largely untouched for years: secretary-certified investigations that can proceed without a formal worker complaint, so long as officials claim there is reasonable cause to believe an employer may be breaking the law.'

notable_text: EVERETT MUNICIPAL BUILDING / Initiative 3: Control and access to city property / no federal agent conducting immigration enforcement shall be allowed to access non-public areas within a city-owned facility
description: A composite graphic combining a photograph with quoted policy text. The top half is a color photograph of the glass-and-wood 'EVERETT MUNICIPAL BUILDING' (its name in large letters on the facade), with red salmon or fish art panels and snow on the ground at the building's base. The bottom half is styled as a torn white sheet of paper (ragged edges top and bottom) overlaid with text in black, headed 'Initiative 3: Control and access to city property', with numbered provisions: '13. Effective immediately, absent a warrant, or other non-immigration related mutual aid investigation in which the Everett Police have sought federal agency assistance, no federal agent conducting immigration enforcement shall be allowed to access non-public areas within a city-owned facility.'; '14. Effective immediately, city employees observing federal agents conducting immigration enforcement or briefing or staging on city owned property shall immediately notify their department director. Department directors shall notify the Mayor's Office or a member of the IRT as soon as practical.'; and '18. Everett police officers who observe an egregious excessive force encounter involving federal immigration officers are expected to take action when it is safe to do so.'

notable_text: JOIN DHS. / FORGE YOUR FUTURE WITH DHS / June 5 - 6, 9 AM - 7 PM ET / Dulles Expo Center, Chantilly, VA / DHS CAREER EXPO / MAKE AN IMPACT.
description: Vertical recruitment poster for the Department of Homeland Security 'DHS Career Expo.' The layout has a navy-blue text panel against angled photographic insets. Top-left small caps read 'JOIN DHS.' The large white bold headline states 'FORGE YOUR FUTURE WITH DHS,' followed by event details set off by red vertical accent bars: 'June 5 - 6 / 9 AM - 7 PM ET' and 'Dulles Expo Center / Chantilly, VA.' At left, two people in tan and khaki gear and helmets descend a thick rope; the text 'POLICE FEDERAL AGENT' is visible on a vest. At bottom right, two people in uniform, life vests and helmets stand atop a low-profile vessel at sea with a ship in the background. The bottom-left shows the round DHS seal beside the 'DHS CAREER EXPO' wordmark, and a bottom banner reads 'MAKE AN IMPACT.' The palette is navy, red and white.

notable_text: Self-deport NOW. / LEAVE ON YOUR OWN TERMS. FREE FLIGHT HOME. $2600 EXIT BONUS. DHS.GOV/CBPHOME / FREE FLIGHT
description: Graphic styled as an airport waiting area. The brightly lit scene shows a row of black-cushioned chrome airport gate seats against a white wall and gray floor; a red hard-shell rolling suitcase with an extended handle stands in front of the seats, bearing a luggage tag printed with CBP Home / DHS branding and 'FREE FLIGHT.' Mounted on the wall is a black mechanical split-flap (Solari) departure-board sign whose white flip-letters spell: 'LEAVE ON YOUR OWN TERMS. FREE FLIGHT HOME. $2600 EXIT BONUS. DHS.GOV/CBPHOME.' Above the board, large bold text reads 'Self-deport' in blue and 'NOW.' in red. The image is a photorealistic 3D composite render with blue and red accents.

notable_text: THE BIDEN JOB MARKET / Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024 / NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000 / FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000
description: Statistics card with a white background and navy serif typography, headlined 'THE BIDEN JOB MARKET' (underlined). A subhead in italic reads 'Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024.' The center holds an embedded FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) bar chart with a legend distinguishing 'Employment Level - Native Born' (light blue bars) from 'Employment Level - Foreign Born' (gray bars), plotted monthly from Aug 2023 to Aug 2024; the native-born bars are mostly below zero (reaching about -2,000 thousand around Dec 2023) and the foreign-born bars are mostly above zero, with footnotes 'Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED' and 'fred.stlouisfed.org.' Below the chart are two large figures in bold navy: 'NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000' and 'FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000.' A footer reads 'Sources: BLS, Federal Reserve Bank, Fox News.'

notable_text: Americans' wages are up 3.8% over the year. Since President Trump took office, workers' real wages are up $700 and are on track for a $1,000 increase after his first year - meaning Americans are on track to make up one-third of the $3,000 in wages lost under Biden.
description: Text-only graphic, white background with dark navy-charcoal sans-serif text occupying the full frame. The first line is bold ('Americans' wages are up 3.8% over the year.') followed by lighter-weight body text reading: 'Since President Trump took office, workers' real wages are up $700 and are on track for a $1,000 increase after his first year - meaning Americans are on track to make up one-third of the $3,000 in wages lost under Biden.' There is no photo, seal, or illustration. The accompanying tweet text reads in part: 'Unrestrained migration under Biden CRUSHED wages for American Workers.'

notable_text: MEXICO. CHILD PREDATOR. ICE in Yakima Washington.
description: Detainee card in portrait format on a near-black background textured with dark topographic contour lines. At top-left, in white bold caps preceded by a red double-chevron '>>', is the tag 'MEXICO'. Centered is a framed booking photograph of a middle-aged man with slicked-back graying hair pulled into a small ponytail, a graying mustache and stubble, deep facial lines, and a flat expression, wearing a gray sweatshirt with a black collar, shot against a plain light wall. Overlaid across the bottom of the photo is a bold red banner with a folded-corner effect bearing white uppercase text 'CHILD PREDATOR'. At the bottom-center is the circular DHS/ICE seal next to the 'ICE' wordmark, the italic word 'in', and the location 'YAKIMA WASHINGTON'. The accompanying tweet identifies the man as Antonio Castaneda-Oseguera and states a strong-arm rape and child-molestation conviction.

notable_text: Total Native-Born Employment Level; Biden Administration vs Trump Administration (so far); Inauguration; THE WHITE HOUSE
description: White House line-chart infographic on a white background titled in navy serif type 'Total Native-Born Employment Level'. A legend shows two colored series: a navy-blue line labeled 'Biden Administration' and a red line labeled 'Trump Administration (so far)'. The y-axis is labeled 'Total employment (thousands)' running from 129,000 to 134,000; the x-axis runs from Nov. 2023 to Nov. 2025. A dashed vertical line marks 'Inauguration' (early 2025). The blue line segment dips and stays roughly flat around 130,000 to 131,000; the red line segment rises to about 133,000 and above. A dark navy footer bar reads 'THE WHITE HOUSE / WASHINGTON'. The tweet text reads: 'Under President Trump, native-born Americans have accounted for ALL NET JOB GAINS. PROMISES MADE, PROMISES KEPT!'

notable_text: Heroic ICE Agents Save Four-Year-Old Boy From Drowning In Minnesota Pool; 'Without the quick response and professional actions [of the officers] the outcome of this event would have likely been tragic.'
description: Graphic showing a screenshot of a Daily Wire+ article composited inside a 3D rendering of a black smartphone (iPhone-style with notch and Dynamic Island) against a gray gradient background. On the phone screen: the 'DAILY WIRE+' logo with a red 'SIGN UP' button and hamburger menu; a red 'EXCLUSIVE' tag; the headline 'Heroic ICE Agents Save Four-Year-Old Boy From Drowning In Minnesota Pool'; a bold pull-quote ''Without the quick response and professional actions [of the officers] the outcome of this event would have likely been tragic.''; the byline 'By Jennie Taer'; 'Feb 25, 2026 DailyWire.com'; social-share icons and '128 Comments'; and the top of a photo showing a hotel pool with green umbrellas and lounge chairs. The tweet text reads 'AMERICAN HEROES' and refers to off-duty agents performing CPR.

notable_text: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST
description: Vertical slogan poster. The background is a photograph of a single American flag flying on a tall flagpole against a blue sky, with bare winter treetops along the bottom. Centered at top is the white line-art seal of the U.S. Department of Labor ('Department of Labor / United States of America'). Below it, large white serif typography reads 'AMERICAN WORKERS' on one line and a larger italic 'FIRST' on the second line. The accompanying tweet describes a proposed rule to reduce incentives to replace American workers with 'low-wage foreign visa labor.'

notable_text: MESSAGE TO ILLEGAL ALIENS: A WARNING TO SELF-DEPORT; Benefits; Consequences; Self-deportation is safe; Keep money earned in the U.S.; Immediate deportation; A fine of $998 per day; Barred from returning
description: A vertical DHS infographic in a red, white, and blue color scheme split into two sections. The header is a blue band with white text: 'MESSAGE TO ILLEGAL ALIENS:' (smaller) above large bold 'A WARNING TO SELF-DEPORT'. The upper (blue) section has a photograph of a hand holding a smartphone displaying the 'CBP Home' app screen (labeled 'Departing Traveler' with a U.S. map and options 'Verify Departure', 'View My Departure', 'Submit Intent To Depart'). Beside it, a white-text list titled 'Benefits:' reads: 'Self-deportation is safe. Leave on your own terms by picking your departure flight. Keep money earned in the U.S. If you self-deport as a non-criminal illegal alien. Future opportunity for legal immigration. If you self-deport using CBP Home instead of being deported by ICE. Possible Flights Home. You may be eligible to a subsidized flight if you cannot afford to leave.' The lower (red) section is titled 'Consequences:' and reads: 'Immediate deportation. You will be apprehended by DHS with no opportunity to get your affairs in order beforehand. Fines and penalties. A fine of $998 per day if you received a final order of removal and stayed. A fine of $1,000 - $5,000 if you fail to self-deport after claiming that you will. Possible imprisonment. If you fail to self-deport, you may be subject to jail time. Barred from returning. Prohibited from reentering the U.S. through the legal immigration system.' The DHS seal appears at lower right.

notable_text: PROGRESS: Jobs Report Shows Private Sector Gains, Wage Growth for American-Born Workers; Under President Trump, all job gains have gone to native-born Americans - reversing the Biden-era trend; 2.5 million native-born Americans gained employment, while 670,000 foreign-born workers lost employment
description: All-text card styled like a press-release or article excerpt. The top portion has a dark navy background with a white serif headline reading 'PROGRESS: Jobs Report Shows Private Sector Gains, Wage Growth for American-Born Workers'. The bottom portion is a light gray and white panel with body text in mixed bold and regular sans-serif: the bold lead-in 'Under President Trump, all job gains have gone to native-born Americans, reversing the Biden-era trend.' followed by 'Over the past year, more than 2.5 million native-born Americans gained employment, while 670,000 foreign-born workers lost employment.' The words 'gained' and 'lost' are underlined. There is no imagery. The headline is in serif type and the body in sans-serif type, in navy and white.

genre_refs: Uncle Sam 'I Want You' WWI recruitment poster (James Montgomery Flagg)
notable_text: DHS recruiting 'patriots' to join ICE, offering up to $50K signing bonus | AMERICA NEEDS YOU | VISIT JOIN.ICE.GOV
description: Vertical graphic. The top portion is a white panel with torn-paper edges containing the ABC affiliate 'KSAT 12' (San Antonio) logo, a red 'LOCAL NEWS' kicker, a bold navy headline 'DHS recruiting 'patriots' to join ICE, offering up to $50K signing bonus', and a subhead 'Multiple incentives are offered as part of the "Defend the Homeland" campaign.' Below a torn edge, on a dark navy background, is a painted illustration of Uncle Sam in a red, white, and blue star-banded top hat and blue coat, pointing his finger toward the viewer (James Montgomery Flagg's image). Across the bottom in large serif capitals is 'AMERICA NEEDS YOU' (with 'YOU' in red), and below it 'VISIT JOIN.ICE.GOV.'

notable_text: Making America Safe Again | If you're here illegally, leave now. | Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.
description: Vertical slogan graphic. The background is a semi-transparent American flag rendered over horizontal wooden boards, in muted red, white and blue tones with a dark blue gradient toward the bottom. Across the lower-center are the dark silhouettes of a group of people (heads and shoulders of several figures), darkened so faces are not visible. In the upper-left corner is a solid blue box with the white serif headline 'Making America Safe Again'. The bottom of the image carries bold white sans-serif text: a large line 'If you're here illegally, leave now.' followed by smaller text 'Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.'

notable_text: If it crosses the U.S. border illegally, it's our job to STOP IT. | PEOPLE MONEY PRODUCTS INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY | ICE.gov
description: Square slogan graphic with a distressed black texture background. Running diagonally up the right side in large white chalk-style stenciled lettering is a stacked list: 'PEOPLE', 'MONEY', 'PRODUCTS', 'INTELLECTUAL', 'PROPERTY'. At upper-left, white sans-serif text reads 'If it crosses the U.S. border illegally, it's our job to', completed by a bold black-on-orange highlighted box reading 'STOP IT.' An orange and yellow painted X mark is brushed over the lower-left of the list. The bottom-left bears the DHS/ICE seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' in white; the bottom-right shows 'ICE.gov'.

notable_text: NEW YORK POST | METRO EXCLUSIVE | Inside ICE operations in New Jersey - as agents nab two child predators
description: A cropped screenshot of a New York Post web article. At the top is the red New York Post masthead bar with a hamburger menu icon, an envelope icon, the white slanted 'NEW YORK POST' logo, and a 'LOG IN' button at right; below it a white navigation row with section tabs 'US News', 'Metro' (red-underlined), 'Long Island', 'Politics', 'World Ne[ws]'. The body shows a red 'METRO' tag and red italic 'EXCLUSIVE' label, followed by the large bold black headline 'Inside ICE operations in New Jersey - as agents nab two child predators.' Beneath are the byline 'By Reuven Fenton and Alex Oliveira' (names in red) and 'Published July 21, 2025, 7:22 p.m. ET', with a small red comment-count badge '61' in the lower right.

notable_text: Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.
description: A horizontal slogan graphic on a pale light-blue background that carries a faint watermark-style circular DHS/USCIS-type seal (the words 'DEPARTMENT' and 'OF' are partly legible around an eagle and Statue of Liberty motif). A large dark-navy serif headline at the upper left and center reads 'Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.', with the word 'privilege' set in italics and underlined by a hand-drawn red swoosh. Below the text are two specimen immigration documents shown at an angle: at left a sample 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA PERMANENT RESIDENT' card bearing the placeholder name 'SPECIMEN / TEST V,' USCIS# 000-000-310, category IR1, country of birth Bahamas, date of birth 17 AUG 1958, with a generic male ID photo over a Statue of Liberty and flag background; at right a larger sample U.S. 'IMMIGRANT VISA' page issued by 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON,' with placeholder holder name 'TRAVELER / HAPPYPERSON,' category IR1, and a machine-readable zone 'VIUSATRAVELER<<HAPPYPERSON<<<...' plus a generic female ID photo.

notable_text: TRUMP'S ECONOMIC WINS CANCEL OUT BIDEN'S LOSSES IN LATEST JOB REPORT (Fox News); AMERICAN-BORN EMPLOYMENT HITS RECORD HIGH AS FOREIGN-BORN JOBS DECLINE (CBS News); TRUMP'S 'ONE BIG BEAUTIFUL BILL' COULD DRIVE A 15% EARNINGS BOOM IN 2026 AS US MANUFACTURING ROARS BACK (Yahoo Finance); APPLE SHIFTS MAC MINI ASSEMBLY FROM ASIA TO U.S. UNDER TRUMP-BACKED 'MAKE IN AMERICA' PLAN (The Economic Times)
description: Headline-collage graphic made to look like a stack of four torn-paper newspaper strips layered over a gray newsprint background. Each strip pairs a media logo with a bold italic black serif headline. From top to bottom: FOX NEWS CHANNEL, 'TRUMP'S ECONOMIC WINS CANCEL OUT BIDEN'S LOSSES IN LATEST JOB REPORT'; CBS NEWS, 'AMERICAN-BORN EMPLOYMENT HITS RECORD HIGH AS FOREIGN-BORN JOBS DECLINE'; YAHOO FINANCE, 'TRUMP'S ‘ONE BIG BEAUTIFUL BILL’ COULD DRIVE A 15% EARNINGS BOOM IN 2026 AS US MANUFACTURING ROARS BACK'; THE ECONOMIC TIMES (ET), 'APPLE SHIFTS MAC MINI ASSEMBLY FROM ASIA TO U.S. UNDER TRUMP-BACKED ‘MAKE IN AMERICA’ PLAN'. The clippings are monochrome with torn edges and outlet logos.

notable_text: Since Jan. 20, HSI has arrested more than 1,000 illegal workers and proposed over $1 million in fines against businesses that exploit and hire illegal workers.; MADISON SHEAHAN, ICE Deputy Director
description: A quote-card graphic on a solid blue gradient background. At the top-center is a circular headshot of a smiling woman with long dark hair in a dark blazer over a white top, in front of an American flag. The center holds a large white serif quote framed by light-blue quotation marks: 'Since Jan. 20, HSI has arrested more than 1,000 illegal workers and proposed over $1 million in fines against businesses that exploit and hire illegal workers.' Beneath it an attribution reads '- MADISON SHEAHAN, ICE Deputy Director'. The lower-right corner bears the DHS seal and the white 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again | If you're here illegally, leave now. | Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.
description: Vertical slogan graphic with a semi-transparent American flag printed over horizontal wooden planks as the background, in muted red, white, and blue tones fading to a dark blue gradient at the bottom. Across the lower-center are dark silhouettes of a group of people (multiple heads and shoulders). The upper-left corner holds a solid blue box with the white serif headline 'Making America Safe Again'. At the bottom, bold white sans-serif text reads, as a large headline, 'If you're here illegally, leave now.' followed by smaller text 'Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.'

genre_refs: Lady Justice / Statue of Justice iconography
notable_text: Not a career. A calling. | ICE.gov/LegalCareers
description: Vertical recruitment poster with a lit bronze statue of Lady Justice (blindfolded, holding a raised sword in one hand and balanced scales in the other, in classical robes), photographed against a blurred interior with sunbeams through windows. The top-left corner carries the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' wordmark in dark text, with 'ICE.gov/LegalCareers' beneath it. The lower-left has a two-line tagline in white serif type: 'Not a career.' (lighter weight) followed by the larger bold 'A calling.' The tweet text refers to ICE's Office of the Principal Legal Advisor, hiring bonuses up to $50,000, and six-figure salaries for government attorneys.

notable_text: Break our laws, lose your privilege.; PERMANENT RESIDENT; VISA; UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
description: Slogan graphic on a light blue background with a faint watermark of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal (text 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY' arced around the edges). At the top, large dark-navy text reads 'Break our laws,' in bold roman type with 'lose your privilege.' below in italic. The lower portion shows two pixelated specimen immigration documents: a sample 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA PERMANENT RESIDENT' green card (surname 'SPECIMEN', given name 'TEST V', USCIS# 000-000-310, Category IR1, with Statue of Liberty and flag artwork) and a sample U.S. immigrant 'VISA' issued by the 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON' (surname 'TRAVELER', Immigrant Visa, IV Case Number LND2004I6000201, Registration Number 00000473). The faces on both documents are obscured.

notable_text: If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VOICE - VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT
description: Graphic for ICE's VOICE program. The right two-thirds is a soft-focus photograph of two pairs of hands clasped together, with a seated person (in a light blue shirt and gray pants). The left third is a blue overlay panel with white text reading 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by 'HELP' in large bold caps. An orange angled shape sits at the right edge. A white footer bar carries contact information in blue and orange: '1-855-48-VOICE' and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE', plus the VOICE logo (a stylized blue 'V') with text 'VOICE, VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT.' This template is also used in 3_1991960082607718400.

notable_text: GANG ARREST; MS-13 La Mara Salvatrucha; Cesar Lopez-Larios; Material support to terrorists; Financing terrorism; Narco-terrorism; Acts of terrorism
description: Square 'GANG ARREST' card. The base is a blurry photograph of a bald, gray-bearded older man standing indoors wearing a dark shirt with a yellow lanyard; the back of another person in a gray shirt is visible at left foreground. Overlaid diagonally across the image in large distressed cream and tan stencil lettering are the words 'GANG' (upper left) and 'ARREST' (lower right, crossing the image at an angle). A dark banner across the top right carries white serif text 'MS-13' with 'La Mara Salvatrucha' beneath it. A semi-transparent dark-red box at lower left contains the name in bold white, 'Cesar Lopez-Larios', followed by a bulleted charge list: 'Material support to terrorists; Financing terrorism; Narco-terrorism; Acts of terrorism.' The DHS seal and 'ICE' wordmark appear at lower right.

notable_text: 'These policy measures are consistent with the Biden-Harris administration's priorities to eliminate unnecessary barriers to our nation’s legal immigration system... a land of opportunity for those who seek it.' - Acting USCIS Director Tracy Renaud
description: Quote-card graphic on a light blue background with a faint, low-opacity Department of Homeland Security seal watermark behind the text. A multi-line quotation is set in dark blue or navy sans-serif text, with the attribution in italic at the lower right. The quotation reads: '“These policy measures are consistent with the Biden-Harris administration's priorities to eliminate unnecessary barriers to our nation’s legal immigration system and reduce burdens on noncitizens who may be eligible for immigration benefits. USCIS is committed to promoting policies and procedures that ensure we operate in a fair, efficient, and humane manner that reflects America’s heritage as a land of opportunity for those who seek it.”' attributed to '- Acting USCIS Director Tracy Renaud'.

notable_text: Break our laws, lose your privilege.
description: Slogan graphic on a light-blue gradient background with a faint large U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal watermark spanning the frame. At the top, a two-line headline in dark navy: 'Break our laws,' (upright) with 'lose your privilege.' (italic) beneath it. Centered below the slogan are two stacked specimen immigration-document images: (top) a sample U.S. 'PERMANENT RESIDENT' card showing surname 'SPECIMEN', given name 'TEST V', USCIS# 000-000-310, category IR1, with a face photo and Statue of Liberty and flag artwork; (bottom) a sample U.S. immigrant 'VISA' issued by 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON', surname 'TRAVELER', IV Case Number LND2004I6000201, Registration Number 00000473, with a face photo. The personal-data fields in both document photos are pixelated.

notable_text: Sharing timely & relevant information with law enforcement partners around the world; LAW ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT CENTER; ICE.gov/LESC
description: An ICE poster in portrait format with a dark desaturated palette. The right side shows a black-and-white and blue-tinted photograph of a young Black man wearing eyeglasses and a telephone headset, viewed in profile; faint blue binary digits (0s and 1s) are overlaid across the dark background. The top-left carries the full-color U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal beside the wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' in white serif. Below it, white serif text reads 'Sharing timely & relevant information with law enforcement partners' with 'around the world.' in blue. The lower-left has a large stacked all-caps title in white and blue: 'LAW ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT CENTER' with red triple-slash accents and the URL 'ICE.gov/LESC'.

notable_text: If you’re the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we’re here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VOICE - Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement
description: ICE VOICE graphic in portrait format. The right portion is a color photograph of two pairs of clasped hands, one person holding another's hand with both of theirs; a seated person in light blue is partially visible. A vertical translucent blue panel overlays the left portion carrying white text: 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by the large word 'HELP'. An orange diagonal shape crosses the lower right. A white footer bar at the bottom contains a contact line in blue and orange: '1-855-48-VOICE' and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE', alongside a blue 'V' VOICE logo and the program name 'VOICE, VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT'. The palette is blue and orange.

notable_text: Don’t intentionally misrepresent facts during your interview; You can lose your status and be removed from the U.S.
description: A poster from USCIS. The top half is a color stock photograph of an interview scene: a man in a grey button-down shirt sits at a table speaking to another person seen from behind (back of a head, white shirt) in a bright office. The bottom half is a solid dark navy panel containing, centered, the full-color U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal above the wordmark 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' in light grey serif text. Below that, in large bold pale-yellow sans-serif type: 'Don’t intentionally misrepresent facts during your interview', and beneath it in white: 'You can lose your status and be removed from the U.S.'

notable_text: First 100 Days Accomplishments; '...the days of exploiting our immigration system are over. Aliens who want to live and work in America need to do it legally or get out.' - USCIS Spokesman Matthew Tragesser
description: A quote card on a white background. Top left: bold navy heading 'First 100 Days' with red italic subhead 'Accomplishments'. Top right: the full-color U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal beside the wordmark 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services'. The body is a large italicized navy serif quotation: '“In the first 100 days, USCIS put a stop to disastrous Biden-era ‘humanitarian’ policies that invited fraud and allowed criminal aliens to legally live and work in our communities; facilitated arrests of criminals attempting to gain immigration benefits; and for the first time in decades is ensuring every alien in the U.S. is registered as required by law,” said USCIS Spokesman Matthew Tragesser. “Aliens, immigration attorneys and non-government organizations take note: the days of exploiting our immigration system are over. Aliens who want to live and work in America need to do it legally or get out.”' At the bottom are red and navy underline rule marks.

notable_text: 13 CHARGED IN ICE HSI & ATF-LED PROBE INTO GUN SMUGGLING RING EXPLOITING THE U.S.-CANADA BORDER
description: Vertical infographic on a black background. The top half has an all-caps white headline in a heavy sans-serif font reading '13 CHARGED IN ICE HSI & ATF-LED PROBE INTO GUN SMUGGLING RING EXPLOITING THE U.S.-CANADA BORDER', with the words 'CHARGED IN' and 'EXPLOITING THE' enlarged. Centered is an inset aerial or satellite map (green and orange shaded regions) with red labeled pins and a red route line marking 'Canadian Border Crossing', 'Akwesasne (Ontario)', 'Akwesasne (New York state)', and 'US Border Crossing'. The bottom portion shows photographs of multiple black semi-automatic handguns laid out, including a gloved hand holding one firearm at lower right. A large faint ghosted 'ICE' watermark spans the lower area, alongside the DHS Homeland Security seal and a small ATF/HSI seal next to bold 'HSI' text.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again; Our borders are closed to criminals. America welcomes those who respect our laws.
description: Vertical slogan graphic with a blue-tinted color photograph of Border Patrol agents riding ATVs (all-terrain quad bikes) in a line, the lead rider wearing a full-face motocross-style helmet, goggles, and a dark uniform jacket with an agency badge on the chest, with additional helmeted agents and a UTV vehicle behind. The photo carries a blue duotone wash. In the upper-left corner a solid blue rectangle holds white bold serif text: 'Making America Safe Again.' Across the lower portion, large bold white sans-serif text reads: 'Our borders are closed to criminals.' followed by 'America welcomes those who respect our laws.'

notable_text: If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VOICE - Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement
description: Vertical, portrait orientation outreach graphic for ICE's VOICE (Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement) office. The right two thirds is a softly lit stock photograph of two people holding hands, one set of hands clasping another's hand, with a seated person in a light blue top partly visible. The left third is a semi-transparent teal and blue panel overlaying the photo, carrying white text: 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by 'HELP' in large bold white caps. An orange diagonal accent shape appears at the right edge. A white footer bar at the bottom contains the contact line '1-855-48-VOICE' (blue, with VOICE bold) and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE' (orange), beside the VOICE logo: a blue 'V' check style mark with 'VOICE' in serif caps over the subtitle 'VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT.'

notable_text: '...stop aliens from hijacking our elections... USCIS is moving quickly to eliminate voter fraud... committed to restoring trust in American elections.' - Matthew Tragesser, Chief of Public Affairs at USCIS
description: A vertical quote card from USCIS on a dark navy textured background with faint oversized quotation-mark graphics behind the text. The body is a white sans-serif quote with selected phrases in bold: 'For years, states have pleaded for tools to help identify and stop aliens from hijacking our elections. Under the leadership of President Trump and Secretary Noem, USCIS is moving quickly to eliminate voter fraud. We expect further improvements soon and remain committed to restoring trust in American elections.' Below, in a small light grey box, the attribution reads 'Matthew Tragesser,' (bold) over 'Chief of Public Affairs at USCIS' (italic). There is no photograph.

notable_text: Protecting AMERICANS; Bloomberg Law; Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer personally signed off on at least one of the probes... tapping into a previously-unused authority to proactively launch investigations not limited by the scope of a complaint.
description: Vertical graphic combining an Oval Office photo with overlaid headline text and an embedded news quote. The top photo shows Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer standing (dark blazer over white blouse) beside President Donald Trump, who is seated at the Resolute Desk smiling in a navy suit and red tie; the Oval Office backdrop includes American and military service flags, the Remington 'Bronco Buster' bronze, framed family photos, and a map of the Americas on an easel at left. Across the lower third of the photo, a headline reads 'Protecting AMERICANS' in a serif font (italic 'Protecting' plus bold all caps 'AMERICANS') in white over a dark band. Below, on a white card, is a black 'Bloomberg Law' logo badge followed by a quoted news excerpt in serif type: 'Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer personally signed off on at least one of the probes, according to an agency spokesperson, tapping into a previously-unused authority to proactively launch investigations not limited by the scope of a complaint.'

notable_text: Providing a fake document to USCIS is fraud; Fraud can get you permanently banned from the U.S.
description: A vertical, portrait-orientation graphic from USCIS. The top half is a color photograph of identity documents spread on a white surface: a dark blue passport (lettered 'PASSPORT'), an open Australian passport photo page with a man's photo and machine-readable 'AUS<<<' code, an Australian ID and driver-license card with a gold EMV chip, several loose passport-style headshot photos of a bearded man, and an open pair of metal scissors lying across the documents. The bottom half is a solid dark navy panel containing the circular DHS Department of Homeland Security seal (eagle crest) above 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' in grey serif type. Below, the headline in bold yellow and cream serif reads 'Providing a fake document to USCIS is fraud,' and beneath it in bold white sans-serif: 'Fraud can get you permanently banned from the U.S.'

notable_text: Making America Safe Again
description: Vertical slogan graphic from USCIS in a blue-and-white duotone treatment. The background photograph shows at left a person in a white shirt with their wrists held behind their back by an officer; an officer in dark tactical clothing stands center; at right a hand holds up a clear plastic bag labeled 'PROPERTY' containing items. A large semi-transparent circular seal (the words 'DEPARTMENT' and a partial ring visible) is overlaid across the lower half as a watermark. In the lower-left corner sits a solid dark-blue rectangle containing white bold serif text on three lines: 'Making America Safe Again.'

notable_text: "For years, states have pleaded for tools to help identify and stop aliens from hijacking our elections... Under the leadership of President Trump and Secretary Noem, USCIS is moving quickly to eliminate voter fraud... restoring trust in American elections." - USCIS Spokesman Matthew Tragesser

The right two-thirds is a photograph of a person's hands typing on a silver laptop keyboard, with a smartphone resting on a wooden desk in the foreground; the person wears a light blue-and-white pinstriped collared shirt. The left third is a darkened, desaturated continuation of the photo used as a backdrop for text. Overlaid on the photo, centered-right, is a large white outline illustration of a document with horizontal text lines and an oversized white checkmark. The background is a stock photo with navy, cyan, and yellow accents.

The brand colors are blue and orange.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again
description: Vertical, portrait-orientation slogan graphic built on a full-color outdoor photograph. In the foreground, an older man with grey hair and beard, wearing a dark short-sleeve shirt, stands with his hands cuffed behind his back. To his right an officer is shown from behind wearing a green camouflage tactical vest with patches reading 'POLICE' and 'ICE,' khaki tactical pants, and a holstered sidearm. The setting is a grassy area with a chain-link fence and green trees. A solid dark-blue rectangle in the left-center contains the slogan in bold white serif text on three lines: 'Making America Safe Again.'

notable_text: Making America Safe Again. If you're here illegally, leave now. Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.
description: Vertical graphic combining a U.S. flag motif with a photographic background. The composition layers a worn American flag on wooden plank slats (red, white, and blue stripes with white stars on a blue field) and superimposes the dark silhouettes of several people, a group including adults and smaller figures, standing in shadow. In the upper-left corner is a solid blue box with white serif text reading 'Making America Safe Again'. Across the lower third, large bold white sans-serif text reads in two parts: a headline 'If you're here illegally, leave now.' followed by 'Return the right way and respect our laws for a chance to live the American dream.' The color palette is red, white, and blue, with the human figures in shadow.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again
description: Vertical, portrait-orientation slogan graphic from USCIS with a red-to-blue duotone gradient overlay on a photograph of a street arrest. From behind, a tactical officer at left wears a ballcap, a face covering, and a vest with patches reading 'POLICE' and 'VIOLENT GANG TASK FORCE'. He holds a handcuffed man whose wrists are cuffed behind his back and who wears a patterned print jacket and camouflage pants, walking along a residential street lined with brick apartment buildings. The left side of the image is red and the upper-right is tinted blue. A solid dark-blue rectangle in the lower-left contains the slogan in white serif text on three lines: 'Making America Safe Again.'

notable_text: Making America Safe Again. USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens.

In the upper-left corner is a solid blue box with white serif text reading 'Making America Safe Again'. The central headline is large bold white sans-serif text: 'USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens'. notable_text: POLICE / ICE (officer's vest). Making America Safe Again.

The photo shows an outdoor scene: in the foreground a man (gray hair and beard, dark shirt) stands with his back to the camera, hands cuffed behind his back, while at right a law-enforcement officer seen from behind wears a green tactical or molle vest with a tan or khaki patch reading 'POLICE / ICE' and tan cargo pants with a holstered sidearm; a chain-link fence and green trees form the background. notable_text: USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens. Making America Safe Again. Analytics Dashboard.
