The bottom half is a photograph of a person's hand at a translucent holographic interface labeled 'Analytics Dashboard' and 'Business Analytics', covered in bar charts, line graphs, pie charts, and figures (for example 'Sales', 'Purchased', 'Total Earning', percentages such as 65%, 25%, 50%). Overlaid at the lower-left is a solid-blue box with white serif text 'Making America Safe Again'.

notable_text: Have you moved? You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.
description: A vertical informational graphic built on a warm, brightly-lit stock photograph of a home interior during a move: in the foreground sits a large brown cardboard moving box printed with a dark moving-truck icon, with more stacked cardboard moving boxes and a wicker basket blurred in the background near a sunlit window, on a wood floor. Overlaid text at top reads, in large bold red serif, the question 'Have you moved?' followed by bold blue serif body text 'You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.' In the lower-right corner is the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services seal (DHS eagle emblem) with blue serif text 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services'. The text is red and blue over the photograph. The accompanying tweet states that failure to comply may result in fines or removal.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again. USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens.
description: A vertical graphic with a dark navy-blue background featuring faint imagery: glowing binary digits (1s and 0s), grid lines, bar-chart and circuit-like motifs, and small blue light particles. In the upper-left corner is a solid blue box with white serif text 'Making America Safe Again'. The central headline is large bold white sans-serif text: 'USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens'. Along the bottom are two seals with white text labels: the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services seal and the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement seal, both with the DHS eagle emblem.

notable_text: ANNOUNCEMENT / Temporary Protected Status / Haiti / Secretary Alejandro N. Mayorkas announces the extension and redesignation of Haiti for Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for 18 months, from August 4, 2024, through February 3, 2026.
description: Horizontal announcement card in DHS brand style on a white background. A blue header bar across the top contains the centered white spaced-out word 'ANNOUNCEMENT,' with the circular DHS seal at the left edge; a thin red rule runs beneath the header. The left portion carries a large bold navy serif headline 'Temporary Protected Status.' The right portion shows a light gray world map graphic with a red-outlined label box reading 'Haiti' and a small red star at Haiti's location in the Caribbean, beside body text: 'Secretary Alejandro N. Mayorkas announces the extension and redesignation of Haiti for Temporary Protected Status (TPS)' (the word redesignation in red) 'for 18 months, from August 4, 2024, through February 3, 2026.'

notable_text: POLICE / ICE (officer's vest). Making America Safe Again.
description: Vertical graphic built on a photograph with a slogan overlay. The photo shows an outdoor arrest scene: in the foreground a gray-haired, bearded man in a dark shirt stands with his back to the camera, hands cuffed behind his back; at right an officer seen from behind wears a green tactical vest with a tan patch reading 'POLICE / ICE' and tan cargo pants with a holstered sidearm; a chain-link fence and green trees form the background. Overlaid on the lower-left is a solid-blue box with white serif text 'Making America Safe Again'. The accompanying tweet describes a Nigerian national with an outstanding warrant for child cruelty and aggravated assault.

notable_text: First 100 Days Accomplishments / Restoring Stronger Safeguards and Commonsense Policies / 7,120 benefit fraud records; 4,664; Screening 3,568 subjects' social media activity; 2,271 site visits; Referring 462 benefit fraud records and 4,672 egregious public safety records to ICE
description: A vertical statistics infographic card on a white background using the USCIS / DHS brand palette of navy blue and red. Top-left header reads 'First 100 Days' in large bold navy type with 'Accomplishments' in red beneath it; the top-right carries the circular DHS seal next to the navy 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark. A centered red bold subhead reads 'Restoring Stronger Safeguards and Commonsense Policies,' followed by an italic navy line 'Some of our screening and vetting efforts since Jan. 20, 2025 include:'. Below is a vertical list of five line-icon entries (folder, magnifying-glass over a document, person in a magnifier, building/warehouse, file folder with shield), each pairing a red bold statistic with navy descriptive text: 'Completing 7,120 benefit fraud records;', 'Identifying fraud in 4,664 number of records;', 'Screening 3,568 subjects' social media activity;', 'Completing 2,271 site visits to workplaces; and', 'Referring 462 benefit fraud records and 4,672 egregious public safety records to ICE for criminal investigation or enforcement.' The icons are flat outline style; the numerals are bold.

notable_text: Wall of Honor. Deportation Officer Frank E. Chaffin. End of Watch. Wednesday, January 28, 1948.
description: 'Wall of Honor' memorial tribute graphic in landscape (1200x675) format. The left portion shows white serif text 'Wall of Honor' over a dark slate blue textured background, with a stylized ICE memorial badge with a blue mourning ribbon (badge reads 'ICE / U.S.'). Below in serif type: 'Deportation Officer / Frank E. Chaffin', a thin gold horizontal rule, 'End of Watch' (with the 'of' in italic), another gold rule, and the date 'Wednesday, January 28, 1948'. The center holds a large square inset of a grainy black-and-white archival portrait photograph of an older man (balding, in a suit and tie). The right side has a photograph of red roses in front of a softly blurred American flag. The tweet notes Chaffin died in a 1948 plane crash while deporting aliens.

notable_text: Tell the truth in your interview / If you lie, you could be banned from the U.S. for life. / U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services
description: Vertical graphic combining a photo with a dark navy text panel. The top two-thirds is a warm-toned photograph of a bearded man wearing glasses and a gray blazer over a white shirt, seated at a light wooden table gesturing with both hands as he speaks, facing a second person seen from behind (dark-haired, in a dark jacket) holding a notepad and pen. Bookshelves are blurred in the background. The bottom third is a solid dark navy blue panel containing, centered, the circular U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal (eagle), the wordmark 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' in gray serif type, then a bold headline in golden-yellow sans-serif reading 'Tell the truth in your interview' followed by white text 'If you lie, you could be banned from the U.S. for life.'

notable_text: There have been almost 47,000 submissions as of April 29 / ARR Alien Registration Requirement
description: Square graphic on a pale gray-blue background, styled to resemble a browser or app window: a rounded-rectangle outline frame in medium blue with three short dashes in the upper left and the DHS seal plus navy 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in the upper right. Inside the frame, large navy text reads 'There have been almost' with '47,000' in bold red and 'submissions as of April 29' completing the line. The lower half holds a large 'ARR' logo: the letters 'ARR' in bold blue with a red-and-blue ribbon curving around the first letter, above the words 'Alien Registration Requirement' (with 'Requirement' in bold). Flat infographic style. The accompanying tweet states that failure to comply may bring fines or removal.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again / POLICE FEDERAL AGENT / POLICE HSI
description: Graphic built from an outdoor photograph with a slogan overlay. The photo shows two officers, seen from behind, escorting a man along a brick-walled walkway with green shrubbery. The two officers wear dark navy jackets with large light lettering across the back: the left officer's jacket reads 'POLICE / FEDERAL AGENT' and the right officer's reads 'POLICE' with 'HSI' partially visible; the right officer's face is blurred. The man in the center wears a dark green pullover and tan pants and has his hands behind his back. Overlaid at the lower right is a solid blue rectangle containing white bold serif text 'Making America Safe Again.'

notable_text: Use the CBP Home app to begin planning your departure and transition back to your home country or another location where you have legal status. / PLAN YOUR DEPARTURE / Welcome To CBP Home / LOGIN OR SIGN UP
description: Graphic in navy blue, cerulean blue, white, and red, split diagonally. The upper-left shows a smartphone mockup displaying the 'Welcome To CBP Home' app screen, labeled 'Official App of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security' with a small U.S. flag, the DHS seal, the text 'A single portal to multiple CBP services to streamline your experience,' and a red 'LOGIN OR SIGN UP' button with a 'with LOGIN.GOV' note. A speech-bubble shape points from the phone to white text on a navy field reading: 'Use the CBP Home app to begin planning your departure and transition back to your home country or another location where you have legal status.' The circular U.S. Customs and Border Protection / DHS seal sits at center. The lower half shows a second smartphone in a white-gloved hand displaying app navigation options, beside a red-tinted photograph of a line of people walking away across tarmac next to an armed officer wearing a vest marked 'POLICE'. Large bold white block text reads 'PLAN YOUR DEPARTURE.'

notable_text: “Bad actors are taking advantage of this humanitarian program. President Trump and Secretary Noem are taking decisive action to make America safe again.” - Matthew Tragesser, Chief of Public Affairs at USCIS
description: Pull-quote graphic on a dark navy-blue background with a large faint open-quotation-mark watermark behind the text. The quote is in light blue and white sans-serif type with some phrases in bold: 'Bad actors are taking advantage of this humanitarian program. President Trump and Secretary Noem are taking decisive action to make America safe again.' Below the quote, a light blue attribution box contains dark text reading 'Matthew Tragesser,' in bold and 'Chief of Public Affairs at USCIS' in italic.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again / USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens
description: Vertical slogan graphic in a dark navy-blue color scheme. The background is an abstract digital or tech motif of faint binary digits (0s and 1s), data streams, bar-chart and circuit-like graphics. In the upper-left corner a solid blue box contains white serif text reading 'Making America Safe Again'. Centered, large bold white sans-serif headline text reads 'USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens.' Along the bottom are two seals with white labels: the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services seal ('U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services') and the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement seal ('U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement').

notable_text: The Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool will help you determine if you need to register. / ARR - Alien Registration Requirement
description: Infographic on a pale blue-gray background for a USCIS digital tool. At top, blue sans-serif text reads 'The Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool will help you determine if you need to register.' Below is a line-art illustration of a web browser window (with three tab marks and a small USCIS and DHS seal plus a 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' label in the corner) containing three interlocking red gear icons. Overlaid is the program's logo: a large blue 'ARR' wordmark wrapped by a red swoosh, with 'Alien Registration Requirement' spelled out beneath.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again / POLICE
description: A slogan graphic over a photograph with a red and blue color grade. The photo, shot from behind, shows a man in a blue hooded sweatshirt and jeans, hands handcuffed behind his back, being escorted by two officers in black tactical vests marked 'POLICE'; one has a visible holstered sidearm. In the lower left, a solid blue box holds bold white serif text: 'Making America Safe Again'.

notable_text: Making America Safe Again
description: A slogan graphic over a sepia-toned photograph. The photo shows a man, back to camera and face not shown, wearing a dark sweater and light khaki pants, with his hands behind his back, held by an officer in a dark jacket whose back reads 'POLICE FEDERAL'. They stand beside a dark SUV in a paved outdoor area; a tree is at upper left. The image is rendered in brown monochrome. In the lower-left, a solid blue rectangle carries large white bold serif text reading 'Making America Safe Again.' No agency seal is visible on the image.

notable_text: Have you moved? You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.
description: Public-information graphic in vertical/square format. The background is a bright photograph of a home interior during a move: a large open cardboard box in the lower-left foreground printed with a dark moving-truck icon, additional stacked boxes and a wicker basket in the mid-ground, a sunlit window behind, and a wood floor. Overlaid text at top reads 'Have you moved?' in large red type, followed by 'You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.' in blue type. The lower-right shows the U.S. Department of Homeland Security circular seal next to the 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in blue. The tweet text states that changing your address with USPS will not update it with USCIS and references penalties.

notable_text: Help USCIS by Reporting Fraud
description: A horizontal banner-style slogan graphic. The background is a dark navy-blue field with a cyan and teal digital fingerprint rendered on the right side, composed of dots and ridge lines. On the left in large white serif type is the headline 'Help USCIS by Reporting Fraud', with the word 'Fraud' set in bold. No additional body text or seal is visible on the image.

notable_text: USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens; Making America Safe Again
description: A vertical headline graphic. The top portion is a light blue panel with a bold dark-blue sans-serif headline: 'USCIS Partnering with ICE to Use Advanced Analytics in Targeting Criminal Aliens'. The lower portion is a stock photograph of a hand using a stylus or finger to touch a glowing translucent 'Analytics Dashboard' interface above a tablet, showing bar charts, line graphs, pie charts and labels such as 'Analytics Dashboard,' 'Business Analytics,' 'Sales,' 'Purchased,' 'Production' and various percentages. In the lower-left corner a solid blue box holds white text 'Making America Safe Again.'

notable_text: BEWARE OF SCAMS and always keep these tips in mind; USCIS will never ask for money or payment over the phone; Always preview a link and check that a URL ends in .gov
description: Vertical designed infographic. The top header is a dark red banner with bold white text 'BEWARE OF SCAMS and always keep these tips in mind:' with a large red exclamation-point graphic on the right. Below are three stacked tips, each with a small icon on the left and text on the right: (1) a computer/monitor icon showing an email with a '.gov' address, 'Make sure emails are sent from addresses ending in .gov'; (2) an illustration of a person on a phone with a red X, 'USCIS will never ask for money or payment over the phone'; (3) a browser/URL icon, 'Always preview a link and check that a URL ends in .gov before clicking on it.' Bottom-left shows the DHS seal and 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark. The background is white with red and dark accents.

notable_text: Have you moved? You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.
description: A public-information graphic. The background is a brightly lit photo of a home mid-move: a large open cardboard moving box in the lower-left foreground printed with a dark moving-truck icon, additional stacked boxes and a wicker basket in the mid-ground, a sunlit window behind, and a wood floor. Overlaid text at top: 'Have you moved?' in large bold red type, then 'You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.' in bold blue type. The bottom-right shows the DHS circular seal beside the 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark. The palette is red and blue. The tweet states that updating with USPS will not update USCIS and that USPS will not forward USCIS mail.

notable_text: The Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool will help you determine if you need to register. ARR - Alien Registration Requirement
description: A graphic on a pale blue background. Top text in blue reads 'The Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool will help you determine if you need to register.' (the phrase 'Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool' is bold). Below is a flat-line illustration of a web browser window with three dots in its top bar and the DHS seal plus the 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in the corner of the browser frame; inside the browser are three interlocking red gear icons. Beneath the browser is a large blue 'ARR' monogram with a red swoosh, captioned 'Alien Registration Requirement.' The style is flat-icon.

notable_text: ONLINE TOOLS; LOCATORS; SELF SERVICE TOOLS; CITIZENSHIP CORNER; RESEARCH TOOLS; ONLINE RESOURCES
description: A square graphic on a white background. Large bold blue centered text reads 'ONLINE TOOLS.' Around the center are small thin-line icons in blue and gray, each labeled, connected by light dotted connector lines and small triangles: a location pin labeled 'LOCATORS' (top-left), a wrench or gear labeled 'SELF SERVICE TOOLS' (top), a flag labeled 'CITIZENSHIP CORNER' (right), a compass labeled 'RESEARCH TOOLS' (lower-right), and a folder or document icon labeled 'ONLINE RESOURCES' (bottom-left). The bottom-right corner shows the DHS seal and 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark.

notable_text: Tell the truth in your interview. If you lie, you could be banned from the U.S. for life.
description: Warning graphic combining a stock photo with a slogan panel. The top two thirds is a photograph of an interview or meeting scene: a bearded man in a gray blazer over a white shirt, wearing glasses, sits at a wooden table gesturing with both hands as he speaks across to another person seen from behind (dark hair, dark jacket) who holds a pen over an open notebook. The bottom third is a solid dark navy panel containing the DHS circular seal, the 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in gray, then a bold gold and yellow headline 'Tell the truth in your interview' and white subtext 'If you lie, you could be banned from the U.S. for life.' The tweet describes the USCIS Denver office facilitating the arrest of a Mexican national wanted for assault, discovered via background checks.

notable_text: Have you moved? You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.
description: Public-information graphic. The background is a bright photo of a room with moving boxes: a large open cardboard box in the lower-left foreground printed with a dark moving-truck icon, additional stacked boxes and a wicker basket in the mid-ground, a sunlit window behind, and a wood floor. Overlaid text at top reads 'Have you moved?' in large red type, then 'You must notify USCIS of your new address within 10 days.' in blue type. The bottom-right shows the DHS circular seal beside the 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark. The palette is red and blue.

notable_text: The Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool will help you determine if you need to register. ARR - Alien Registration Requirement
description: A graphic for the USCIS Alien Registration Requirement tool. Pale blue background. Top text in dark/medium blue reads 'The Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool will help you determine if you need to register.' (with 'Alien Registration Requirement Determination Tool' bolded). Below is a flat-line illustration of a browser window with three dots in its top bar and the DHS seal plus 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' wordmark in its corner; inside are three interlocking red gear icons. Beneath sits the program logo: a large blue 'ARR' monogram with a red swoosh, captioned 'Alien Registration Requirement.' Flat-icon style.

notable_text: Register for a free USCIS online account and explore the benefits today! File Online with USCIS. Get started at uscis.gov/file-online.
description: Flat-design graphic on a solid USCIS-blue background, in landscape (1200x630) format. The left side carries white sans-serif text in mixed weights: 'Register for a' (regular), 'free USCIS online account' (bold), 'and explore the benefits today!' (regular), then in white italic 'File Online with USCIS', and at lower-left 'Get started at uscis.gov/file-online.' (with the URL bolded). The right side has a white line-art illustration of an open laptop displaying a login screen: a circular user icon, a 'NAME' field, and a password field showing asterisks, with two rounded button shapes below. A thin white connector-line path with small open circles links the laptop to the text block. There is no photography, no people, and no agency seal beyond the USCIS wordmark in the text.

notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
description: A comparison-style infographic on a dark black asphalt or stone texture background, with a pair of metal handcuffs near the bottom center. The headline at top in white sans-serif (with 'law enforcement officers' in bold) reads: 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Below are two columns separated by a vertical divider. The left column has a red banner labeled 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' with a large white figure '8 to 10' and caption: 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' The right column has a blue banner labeled 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' with a large white figure '2' and caption: 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' Bottom left shows the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' text; bottom right reads 'ICE.gov/detainers'.

notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/27/2025 | Houston, Texas; Jose Guadalupe Garza; CRIME: Aggravated assault with deadly weapon; ICE; HOUSTON
description: An ICE card with the headline 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL'. The full-bleed background is a high-contrast black-and-white close-up of a hand locked in steel handcuffs, palm open, with the cuff and chain on the right side. A dark overlay covers the left column and top band. The headline is in bold pale-cyan serif capitals, with white subhead '1/27/2025 | Houston, Texas'. The subject's name 'Jose Guadalupe Garza' is in large bold white sans-serif, followed by 'CRIME:' and a bullet 'Aggravated assault with deadly weapon.' The white DHS/ICE seal and 'ICE' wordmark are in the lower-left corner; a diagonal red banner in the lower-right reads 'HOUSTON'.

notable_text: JUNE 20, 2024; World Refugee Day
description: Horizontal observance-day graphic with a pale-yellow background. Centered top text in grey sans-serif reads 'JUNE 20, 2024', below it in large blue serif italic-styled text 'World Refugee Day.' The lower center has a flat illustrated planet Earth (blue oceans, green continents showing North and South America) ringed by an arc of brown and tan footprint and shoe-print symbols around the globe. The white DHS (Department of Homeland Security) seal sits in the bottom-right corner.

notable_text: TdA ARREST; Gianfranco Augusto Barazarte-Morales; ASSAULT, WEAPON POSSESSION & DRUG DISTIRBUTION; Tren de Aragua
description: Tren de Aragua arrest card in ICE's template (square). A blue header bar reads 'TdA ARREST' in large gold serif caps. The center background is an in-custody photograph of a young man in a dark green hooded jacket against an institutional wall, looking at the camera. At left in bold white sans-serif is the name across four lines: 'Gianfranco Augusto Barazarte-Morales.' A red banner across the lower center lists charges in white caps: 'ASSAULT, WEAPON POSSESSION & DRUG DISTIRBUTION' (the word 'DISTIRBUTION' is spelled that way). A bottom blue footer bar shows the circular DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' (white) at left and 'Tren de Aragua' in gold serif at right.

notable_text: 2,700 criminal alien arrests in New York State; U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement
description: Statistics card with a large black-and-white photographic portrait of a Black woman speaking into two microphones at a lectern; she wears a dark square-neck dress and a multi-strand pearl necklace (the tweet identifies New York Attorney General Letitia James). On the left, a semi-transparent navy panel holds a large gold serif numeral '2,700' over white serif text 'criminal alien arrests in New York State.' The bottom is a solid navy band with the circular DHS seal and the white text 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.' The tweet states she wants to 'let them roam free.'

notable_text: ICE; A 19-year-old Bangladeshi migrant reportedly died in ICE custody in El Paso, according to witnesses... This would be the first death recorded since the facility opened; Migrante de bangladesh de 19 anos habria muerto bajo custodia de ICE en El Paso, segun testigos; NOPE.
description: A card on a dark navy background. At the top is the white DHS seal and bold white serif 'ICE' wordmark. Below, in white serif text: 'A 19-year-old Bangladeshi migrant reportedly died in ICE custody in El Paso, according to witnesses. A migrant has reportedly died at the Fort Bliss detention center. This would be the first death recorded since the facility opened.' Centered is an embedded screenshot of a Univision 'noticias EL PASO' webpage with the Spanish headline 'Migrante de bangladesh de 19 anos habria muerto bajo custodia de ICE en El Paso, segun testigos' and subhead 'Presunta muerte de migrante en centro de detencion de Fort Bliss. Seria el primer fallecimiento registrado desde su apertura,' tagged 'NOTICIAS / INMIGRACION.' The bottom has a torn-newspaper texture strip over which is set a large bold navy word 'NOPE.' followed by a row of red chevron arrows pointing right.

notable_text: Worst of the Week; ice.gov/WoW; Sexual Assault; Child Abuse; Child Rape; Fentanyl Possession; Rape; Child Sexual Exploitation; ICE
description: A 'Worst of the Week' graphic on a black textured background. Twelve booking-photo-style photographs of men are arranged in a grid (four across the top, single columns down the left and right of a central title, and four across the bottom). Each headshot has a gray caption bar beneath it: 'Sexual Assault,' 'Aggravated Assault,' 'Child Abuse,' 'Child Sexual Assault,' 'Child Rape,' 'Fentanyl Possession,' 'Cocaine Possession,' 'Rape,' 'Indecency w/ Child,' 'Aggravated Assault,' 'Firearm Possession,' 'Child Sexual Exploitation.' Centered is a white logo lockup reading 'Worst' with a red parallelogram banner 'of the' and 'Week.' At the bottom left is a red bar with white text 'ice.gov/WoW'; the bottom right shows the circular DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: DOMESTIC VIOLENCE; Worst of the Worst; San Diego; ICE
description: A 'Worst of the Worst' card styled as a Polaroid instant photo taped to a dark brick wall. The white-framed Polaroid contains a booking/detainee photo of a man with short black hair, a mustache and a small soul-patch beard, wearing a dark jacket, seated in an office; on the white lower border of the Polaroid is handwritten-style black text 'DOMESTIC VIOLENCE.' Below the photo is bold white serif 'Worst' with a red parallelogram 'of the' and 'Worst,' a thin vertical divider, and the location 'San Diego' in white. At the bottom center are the DHS seal and the white 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: PARTNER PROGRAMS; Jail Enforcement Model; Task Force Model; Warrant Service Officer; ICE.gov/287g
description: A square dark-navy infographic card about ICE's three local and state law-enforcement partnership models under the 287(g) program. The background is a mottled dark blue textured field. At top, white serif text reads 'ICE local and state law enforcement' above a large white serif headline 'PARTNER PROGRAMS'. Down the left side are three rounded-rectangle thumbnail photos, each tinted a different color: the top image (red tint) shows cell bars with an officer; the middle image (blue tint) shows officers in tactical vests; the bottom image (grayscale) shows a hand writing with a pen on documents. To the right of each thumbnail is a bold white subhead and body text: 'Jail Enforcement Model - ID and process removable aliens you arrest with active or pending criminal charges.'; 'Task Force Model - Allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority in certain situations.'; 'Warrant Service Officer - Serve and carry out administrative warrants against illegal aliens.' Bottom-left reads 'ICE.gov/287g' in white; bottom-right shows the DHS seal and the wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'.

notable_text: IT'S TIME TO GET RIGHT WITH THE LAW; ICE.gov/IMAGE
description: Portrait-format slogan graphic over a color photograph of a commercial restaurant kitchen. The photo shows a woman in a gray cap, white T-shirt, and plastic apron at a stainless-steel range, holding a frying pan with food; a second worker in a cap and apron stands at right with stacked dishes, among steel pots, burner knobs, and steam. Overlaid at upper left in a distressed stencil typeface is 'IT'S TIME TO' (white), 'GET RIGHT' (yellow), 'WITH THE' (white), 'LAW' (large yellow). Bottom-left in white is the URL 'ICE.gov/IMAGE'; bottom-right shows the round DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: GANG ARREST; PCC - Primeiro Comando da Capital; Pedro Oscar De Souza-Almeida; Weapons sales and possession; ICE
description: Square card. The words 'GANG' and 'ARREST' run diagonally across the image in large, distressed, cream-colored stencil lettering. The background is a darkened, blurred photograph of several men sitting outdoors on a bench. The upper-right header reads 'PCC' in large white serif capitals with 'Primeiro Comando da Capital' beneath it. A semi-transparent red panel at lower left carries a name in white sans-serif, 'Pedro Oscar De Souza-Almeida,' with a bulleted charge: 'Weapons sales and possession.' The lower right shows the circular DHS seal and 'ICE' wordmark in white serif.

notable_text: Sharing timely & relevant information with law enforcement partners around the world. LAW ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT CENTER  ICE.gov/LESC
description: Vertical poster. The background is a desaturated black-and-white close-up photograph of a young Black man in profile wearing a telephone headset with boom mic and eyeglasses, with a faint blue digital '1s and 0s' overlay across the dark left side. Top-left carries the blue-and-white DHS seal and the text 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.' Mid-left, in white serif type with the last line in blue, reads: 'Sharing timely & relevant information with law enforcement partners around the world.' The lower-left has a large bold sans-serif lockup 'LAW ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT CENTER' (with 'SUPPORT' in blue and red triple-slash marks) followed by the URL 'ICE.gov/LESC'. The image is monochrome with blue and red accent type.

notable_text: TdA ARREST; Yerbis Manuel Garcia-Quintero; KIDNAPPING, FALSE IMPRISONMENT, ROBBERY AND ASSAULT WITH A WEAPON, EXTORTION & WITNESS INITIMIDATION; Tren de Aragua
description: Square arrest card. A blue header bar reads 'TdA ARREST' in large gold serif caps. The center background is a photograph of a man with wavy dark hair in an orange jail uniform against a cinderblock wall, looking at the camera. Overlaid at left in bold white sans-serif is the name across four lines: 'Yerbis Manuel Garcia-Quintero.' A red banner across the lower portion lists charges in white caps: 'KIDNAPPING, FALSE IMPRISONMENT, ROBBERY AND ASSAULT WITH A WEAPON, EXTORTION & WITNESS INITIMIDATION' (the word 'INITIMIDATION' is spelled that way). A bottom blue footer bar shows the circular DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' (white) at left and 'Tren de Aragua' in gold serif at right.

notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/26/2025 | St. Paul, MN; Octavio Juarez Bonilla; CRIME: Possession of child pornography; ICE; ST. PAUL
description: Card with a full-bleed grainy black and white photograph of a man with a shaved or short-haired head and a goatee, shown from the chest up, indoors with a plant behind him; the photograph is low-resolution. A dark overlay covers the upper band and the left column. In bold pale-cyan serif capitals: 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL'. In white beneath it: '1/26/2025 | St. Paul, MN'. In large bold white sans-serif: 'Octavio Juarez Bonilla', followed by 'CRIME:' and a bullet reading 'Possession of child pornography.' The white DHS and ICE seal and the 'ICE' wordmark are in the lower left. A diagonal red banner in the lower right reads 'ST. PAUL'.

notable_text: DAILY WIRE + ; EXCLUSIVE; ICE's Most Heroic Moments Of 2025; "Officers show up knowing the risks they face - and they choose to serve anyway."
description: Vertical graphic co-branded with Daily Wire+. The top half is a torn-paper, off-white textured background carrying the black 'DAILY WIRE +' wordmark (with a small red plus sign) above a thin red rule reading 'EXCLUSIVE', followed by the serif headline 'ICE's Most Heroic Moments Of 2025' and a bold quote: '"Officers show up knowing the risks they face, and they choose to serve anyway."' The bottom half, separated by a ragged torn-paper edge, is a color-graded nighttime photograph in blue and purple light: a masked officer in body armor wearing a vest marked 'POLICE' walks beside a handcuffed man (hands cuffed behind his back, gray jacket, jeans, ball cap) along a road past SUVs with flashing red and blue lights, with other officers in the background.

The top-left headline is light-cyan bold sans-serif 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL', with a white sub-line '2/6/2025 | Omaha, NE.' notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "I think of all the sacrifices my parents made just for the hope of having a different life...how incredibly brave and resilient my parents were to leap into this life with that hope."; Myung K., TSA, Acting Chief of Staff, 2nd generation from South Korea
description: DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' card: deep navy-blue background with a faint large American-flag watermark and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular black and white photograph of four people (an older woman, a younger woman, a man in glasses behind, and another woman in front) embracing outdoors; they are smiling. Below a large white quotation mark, the white text reads: "I think of all the sacrifices my parents made just for the hope of having a different life. Just a dream and idea. They did not know how things were going to work out for our family. I think of how incredibly brave and resilient my parents were to leap into this life with that hope." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left reads: 'Myung K. / TSA, Acting Chief of Staff / 2nd generation from South Korea.' The circular DHS seal is at bottom-right.

notable_text: With self-deportation, you have choices; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
description: Portrait-format graphic. The image shows three young people seen from behind (two with backpacks and hair tied up, one with short dark hair) facing a warm-toned world map or globe rendered in orange and blue tones with a sun flare. A dark gradient overlay at the bottom carries white and light-blue text: 'With self-deportation, you have' in white sans-serif, followed by a large light-blue script word 'choices'. A solid dark-blue footer bar with a pinstripe pattern contains the round DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark on the left, a vertical divider, and 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation' on the right.

notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "[Immigrant Heritage Month] reminds me where I came from and the struggles and sacrifices that my parents had to endure..."; Alexander M., CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer, 1st generation from Albania
description: DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' card: deep navy-blue background with a faint large American-flag watermark and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular photograph of a bearded man in a dark CBP uniform wearing a trooper hat, arms crossed, standing in an indoor hall in front of a stone wall engraved with names. Below a large white quotation mark, the white text reads: "[Immigrant Heritage Month] reminds me where I came from and the struggles and sacrifices that my parents had to endure so that I have a better future. Being an immigrant can be very challenging since you have to adjust to different cultures and people, but it also makes you resilient, humble, and able to overcome any difficult situation." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left reads: 'Alexander M. / CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer / 1st generation from Albania.' The circular DHS seal is at bottom-right.

notable_text: ICE local and state law enforcement PARTNER PROGRAMS; Jail Enforcement Model; Task Force Model; Warrant Service Officer; ICE.gov/287g
description: Vertical infographic on a dark navy-blue distressed textured background listing ICE's 287(g) partnership models. The top header reads, in small caps then large white serif: 'ICE local and state law enforcement PARTNER PROGRAMS'. Down the left edge are three rounded thumbnail photos, each color-tinted: a red-tinted image of hands gripping cell bars, a blue-tinted image of officers in 'POLICE/SHERIFF' marked gear, and a grayscale image of a hand signing a document. To the right of each is a bold serif heading with text: 'Jail Enforcement Model - ID and process removable aliens you arrest with active or pending criminal charges.' / 'Task Force Model - Allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority in certain situations.' / 'Warrant Service Officer - Serve and carry out administrative warrants against illegal aliens.' Bottom-left has the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/287g'.

notable_text: Worst of the Week; ice.gov/WoW; crime labels: Child Sex Abuse, Manslaughter, Robbery, Stalking, Drug Possession, Aggravated Assault, Homicide, Attempted Murder
description: Designed 'Worst of the Week' collage card in portrait format on a black textured background. Twelve booking-style photographs of men are arranged in a grid (four columns, three rows, with the center positions occupied by the title block). Each photograph has a small dark caption bar beneath it naming a crime: top row 'Child Sex Abuse', 'Manslaughter', 'Robbery', 'Stalking'; middle 'Drug Possession', then the logo, then 'Child Sex Abuse'; next rows 'Aggravated Assault' and 'Manslaughter'; bottom row 'Homicide', 'Child Sex Abuse', 'Manslaughter', 'Attempted Murder'. The men are of varied ages, photographed against booking backgrounds (one shows a height-measurement scale). The centerpiece is large white serif and script lettering 'Worst of the Week' with 'of the' set on a red banner. A bottom-left red bar contains the URL 'ice.gov/WoW' in white, and the bottom-right shows the round DHS seal and 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: Univision reported that this 19-year-old illegal alien died in custody Oct. 20. He's still in custody and very much alive...; Bottom line: This is just dangerous journalism and misinformation.
description: Text-statement card, the second image in a two-image thread. On a dark navy background with a torn-newspaper texture strip along the bottom, the white DHS seal and bold white serif 'ICE' wordmark sit at top center. The body is a thin-bordered box containing a multi-paragraph white serif statement: 'Univision reported that this 19-year-old illegal alien died in custody Oct. 20. He's still in custody and very much alive, sitting in medical, and claiming to be a juvenile. Following a fainting episode described as a seizure, another alien witness must have called a family member who then called the media. The media states that he is presumed dead, likely because he was getting medical care away from other detainees. Bottom line: This is just dangerous journalism and misinformation.'

notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "My immigrant story starts with my father immigrating to the United States in 1947...he started out in Idaho as a sheepherder...returned to his hometown in the Basque country to find a wife..."; Stacey L., TSA, Transportation Security Officer, 2nd generation from Spain
description: Graphic with a deep navy-blue background, a faint large American-flag watermark, and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular color photo of three women posing together; the middle woman wears a black cap and gown and holds a diploma, flanked by two other women in casual and business-casual dress, standing outdoors near a storefront. Below a large white quotation mark, the white text reads: "My immigrant story starts with my father immigrating to the United States in 1947. He immigrated to the American West for a job and a better life. He started out in Idaho as a sheepherder. Later on, after three years, he found a good job working for Kennecott Copper Mine in Utah. In 1959 he returned to his hometown in the Basque country to find a wife. He met and married my mother and brought her back to the United States where they had five children." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left reads: 'Stacey L. / TSA, Transportation Security Officer / 2nd generation from Spain.' The circular DHS seal is at bottom-right.

description: A designed 'Worst of the Week' rogues-gallery collage card in portrait format on a black textured background. Twelve booking-style mugshots of individuals are arranged in a grid (four columns, three rows), with the center two slots occupied by the title block and one by the agency seal. Each photo has a caption bar naming an alleged crime: top row 'DUI w/ a Child', 'Drug Possession', 'Rape', 'Fraud'; second row 'Distributing Controlled Substance', then title, then 'Child Abuse'; third row 'Stalking', then title, then 'Poisoning'; bottom row 'DUI', 'Assault w/ Firearm', 'Attempted Murder', 'Obscenity to a Child'. The subjects appear to be of varied ages, ethnicities and include at least one woman (captioned 'DUI'), photographed against jail/booking backgrounds. The centerpiece is large white serif branding 'Worst of the Week' with 'of the' on a red banner. A bottom-left red bar shows the URL 'ice.gov/WoW' in white; bottom-right shows the round DHS seal and 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: CBP | Thank you to our CBP workforce for safeguarding our borders and securing lawful trade and travel. | #5DaysofThanks
description: Landscape graphic. The right portion is a color photograph of a woman seated in a booth or vehicle. She wears a dark ball cap embroidered 'CBP', a black jacket with a 'POLICE OFFICER' nameplate (the name 'V. Rodriguez' is partially visible) and a badge, and she is holding a shoulder radio microphone near her mouth. A semi-transparent green block covers the left portion. The letters 'CBP' appear in large green capitals across the top. A green horizontal banner in the lower left carries white text: 'Thank you to our CBP workforce for safeguarding our borders and securing lawful trade and travel.' Below it, in black text, is '#5DaysofThanks'. The circular blue U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal is in the bottom-right corner.

notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers
description: Vertical infographic. The background is a dark charcoal-black asphalt-like texture, with a faint photograph of a pair of dark metal handcuffs lying in the lower center. The white headline at top reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Below are two angled banner labels: a red banner 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the left and a blue banner 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the right, separated by a vertical divider. Under the red side is the numeral '8 to 10' with body text: 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' Under the blue side is the numeral '2' with body text: 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' Bottom-left has the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers'.

notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "My entire life in the U.S. is dedicated to showing my gratitude for the opportunity to live in the greatest country on Earth..."; Yahor S., CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer, 1st generation from Republic of Belarus
description: DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' spotlight card with a deep navy-blue background and a faint large American flag as a watermark. The white headline at top reads 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month.' Centered is a rectangular portrait of a man in a dark CBP (Customs and Border Protection) uniform with a badge, standing in front of an American flag and a second flag, name tape partly visible. Below a large white quotation mark is text in white serif: "My entire life in the U.S. is dedicated to showing my gratitude for the opportunity to live in the greatest country on Earth. Every day, I am thankful for the chance and the green card we received in 2013. I have achieved a lot and aim to achieve even more, contributing to making the U.S. stronger and more resilient." A blue rounded banner at bottom-left reads: 'Yahor S. / CBP, Customs and Border Protection Officer / 1st generation from Republic of Belarus.' The circular DHS seal is at bottom-right.

notable_text: CECOT - CENTRO DE CONFINAMIENTO DEL TERRORISMO; “President Trump and I have a clear message to criminal illegal aliens: LEAVE NOW. If you do not leave, we will hunt you down, arrest you, and you could end up in this El Salvadoran prison.”; Secretary Kristi Noem, U.S. Department of Homeland Security
description: A quote card graphic. The top two-thirds is a color photograph of Kristi Noem standing in front of concrete signage; behind her a wall reads 'CECOT' in large gray letters and 'CENTRO DE CONFINAMIENTO DEL TERRORISMO' beneath. Noem wears a navy ball cap with a gold DHS badge and a white long-sleeve top, and looks off to the side. A red horizontal divider separates the photo from a navy-blue lower panel containing a white bold-serif quote: '“President Trump and I have a clear message to criminal illegal aliens: LEAVE NOW. If you do not leave, we will hunt you down, arrest you, and you could end up in this El Salvadoran prison.”' Below a short white rule it is attributed to 'Secretary Kristi Noem / U.S. Department of Homeland Security,' with the white DHS seal in the lower-left.

notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday - the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation

One person wears a denim jacket, a face mask, and a shoulder bag and hugs another in a white sleeved top. White sans-serif text on the lower left reads: 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday -' followed by a large serif headline 'the LEGAL way' with 'LEGAL' highlighted in bright green. A dark blue band across the bottom carries the DHS seal and the 'ICE' wordmark on the left and 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation' on the right.

notable_text: RESCUE; U.S. Customs and Border Protection
description: A widescreen photograph overlaid with bold text. The photo shows several Border Patrol and CBP agents in green and camouflage tactical uniforms and ball caps carrying a person on an orange rescue stretcher along a dirt agricultural road lined with green brush; in the background a white-and-red ambulance marked '911' waits with additional first responders, one wheeling an empty gurney. One agent at right wears blue medical gloves. The word 'RESCUE' is overlaid in very large bold white all-caps sans-serif across the left-center of the image. The lower-right corner carries the circular DHS and CBP eagle seal and the wordmark 'U.S. Customs and Border Protection.'

notable_text: Eligibility and Process; To be considered on a case-by-case basis for this process, an individual must: be present in the United States without admission or parole; continuously present at least 10 years as of June 17, 2024; legally valid marriage to a U.S. citizen as of June 17, 2024; no disqualifying criminal history.
description: A square text-based graphic in DHS branding with a dark navy-blue background and a large faint watermark of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security circular seal. A centered white serif heading reads 'Eligibility and Process' underlined by a short red rule. Below, a white serif subhead reads 'To be considered on a case-by-case basis for this process, an individual must:' followed by four white bulleted points: 'Be present in the United States without admission or parole;' 'Have been continuously present in the United States for at least 10 years as of June 17, 2024;' 'Have a legally valid marriage to a U.S. citizen as of June 17, 2024; and' 'Have no disqualifying criminal history or otherwise constitute a threat to national security or public safety and should otherwise merit a favorable exercise of discretion.'

notable_text: Keep your community safer - Partner with ICE. POLICE ICE / SHERIFF. ICE.gov/287g
description: Vertical poster in a duotone treatment. Two officers are shown from behind, their backs filling the frame: the left officer wears a ball cap and a tactical vest with white block lettering 'POLICE / ICE'; the right officer wears a tactical vest reading 'SHERIFF'. The background is red-and-blue lighting (red on the left, deep blue on the right with a bluish-white burst at center). Across the lower third in white type: 'Keep your community safer -' (lighter weight) above a large bold serif 'Partner with ICE'. The bottom-left bears the circular DHS seal and the 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' wordmark; the bottom-right shows the URL 'ICE.gov/287g'.

notable_text: 287(g) Participating Agencies; ICE OPERATES THREE 287(g) MODELS: Jail Enforcement Model / Task Force Model / Warrant Service Officer; Participating vs Pending
description: Map infographic on a white/light-blue gradient background showing a gray silhouette map of the contiguous United States covered with colored dots clustered in the Southeast (especially Florida and Texas). The top left has the DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'. A left-hand text block titled 'ICE OPERATES THREE 287(g) MODELS:' reads: 'The Jail Enforcement Model allows your officers to identify and process removable aliens who have pending or active criminal charges.'; 'The Task Force Model allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority while performing routine police duties or as an active participant in an ICE-led task force.'; 'The Warrant Service Officer allows your officers to serve and execute administrative warrants on aliens in your custody.' A large title near the lower left reads '287(g) Participating Agencies' (with '287(g)' in a blue box). A legend at the bottom distinguishes 'Participating' agencies (solid red = Jail Enforcement Model, solid blue = Task Force Model, solid green = Warrant Service Officer) from 'Pending' agencies (lighter versions of each).

Below the photo, large white serif type gives the name 'Javier Ulises Sanchez-Andazola', followed by smaller white text 'CONVICTED OF SEXUAL ASSAULT AGAINST A CHILD AND DUI IN GREELEY.' (with the offense phrases bolded). Beneath that, spaced-out faint capitals read 'DENVER', and at the bottom is the DHS seal next to the white wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.'

Beneath, a bold white headline reads 'Making America Safe Again' with a subhead: 'The United States Southern Border is safer TODAY than any other day in American history.' The center is a bar chart titled 'Monthly Total Encounters at the Southern Border' with a y-axis from 0 to 140,000. A tall red bar for 'March 2024' is labeled '137,473' and has a small circular cut-out photo of President Biden's face at its base; a short green bar for 'March 2025' is labeled '7,180'. A large green downward curving arrow runs from the top of the Biden bar to the 2025 bar, with a circular cut-out photo of President Trump's face and a '95%' down-arrow label. A footnote reads '*CBP data from March 1 - 30, 2024 versus 2025.' The white DHS seal sits in the lower right.

notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 2/11/2025 | New Orleans, LA; Dany Belkis Vasquez-Rodriguez; CRIMES: Fentanyl trafficking conspiracy; THE WORST FIRST; NEW ORLEANS
description: Square arrest card graphic from ICE's 'THE WORST FIRST' branded series. The background is a high-contrast black-and-white close-up photograph of a person's hand and wrist locked in a metal handcuff, with the open palm facing up. A red diagonal banner across the top right corner reads 'NEW ORLEANS' in white serif text. The upper left carries a light blue (cyan) serif headline 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL' with a white sub-line '2/11/2025 | New Orleans, LA'. The subject's name is set large in bold white sans-serif over the photo: 'Dany Belkis Vasquez-Rodriguez'. Below it, 'CRIMES:' in bold white with a bullet: 'Fentanyl trafficking conspiracy.' The lower left shows the DHS seal and white 'ICE' wordmark. The lower right is a red box containing the program logotype '-THE- WORST FIRST' in white serif type.

notable_text: DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month; "Although I am very Americanized in many ways, I am still fluent in my native tongue Ga..."; Alvin M., FEMA, Contract Specialist, 1st generation from Ghana
description: DHS 'National Immigrant Heritage Month' spotlight card matching the series template: deep navy blue background with a faint large American flag watermark, and the white bold headline 'DHS Celebrates National Immigrant Heritage Month' at top. Centered is a rectangular portrait of a smiling young Black man in a dark suit, white shirt and dark tie, standing in front of an American flag and a second (state or agency) flag. Below a large white quotation mark, the white testimonial reads: "Although I am very Americanized in many ways, I am still fluent in my native tongue Ga. Speaking my native country's dialect and eating our native foods brings me a deep sense of connection to the many generations of my family that came before me." A blue rounded banner at the bottom left gives the attribution: 'Alvin M. / FEMA, Contract Specialist / 1st generation from Ghana.' The circular DHS seal sits at the bottom right.

notable_text: MOST WANTED; ANONYMOUS TIP LINE 866-DHS-2-ICE
description: 'MOST WANTED' card in a square format. A bold red banner across the top carries the white serif headline 'MOST WANTED'. The center is filled with a booking style color photograph of a man with short dark hair, thin eyebrows, a mustache and a goatee, wearing an orange jail jumpsuit, photographed against a plain pale blue-gray wall and looking slightly off camera. A matching red banner across the bottom reads 'ANONYMOUS TIP LINE' above the large white number '866-DHS-2-ICE', with the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' text to the right. Per the tweet this is ICE Most Wanted fugitive Ismael Ortega Hernandez (homicide warrant in Arizona, a Mexican national).

notable_text: Student and Exchange Visitor Program; Government agencies needing information about aliens coming to the U.S. primarily as students can contact us at: ICE.gov/SEVP
description: Square promotional graphic for ICE's Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP). The background is a stylized photo of a young person with a backpack seen from behind, silhouetted against a hazy sunset sky, facing a billowing American flag on a pole at left. Overlaid in large white serif type at upper right is the headline 'Student and Exchange Visitor Program' (with 'and' smaller). Beneath it, smaller white sans-serif body text reads: 'Government agencies needing information about aliens coming to the U.S. primarily as students can contact us at:' followed by a blue angular banner reading 'ICE.gov/SEVP' (with SEVP in orange). The lower right carries the circular blue DHS seal and the wordmark 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement.'

notable_text: On this day, In 2012, President Obama launched DACA (Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals).
description: Wide (1200x675) 'On this day' infographic card. The left two-thirds is an off-white and pale-blue background carrying black text: a large serif headline 'On this day,' followed by sans serif body text 'In 2012, President Obama launched DACA (Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals).' Along the bottom runs a horizontal line in a yellow-to-green-to-blue gradient punctuated by open circular node markers. The right third is an angled photographic inset showing Barack Obama in a dark suit standing in the Oval Office beside a young woman in a black blazer and pink top, with another person in the background. The blue and white U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal sits in the lower-right corner over the photo.

notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday - the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
description: A graphic built over a soft-focus stock photograph of two people embracing in what appears to be an airport terminal (blurred travelers and ceiling lights in the background). One person, wearing a denim jacket and a face mask and carrying a shoulder bag, hugs another person dressed in a white sleeved top. Over the left/lower portion of the image, white sans-serif text reads: 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday -' followed by a very large serif headline 'the LEGAL way' where the word 'LEGAL' is rendered in bright green. A dark blue band across the bottom carries the DHS seal and the large 'ICE' wordmark on the left, and on the right: 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation'.

notable_text: ICE local and state law enforcement PARTNER PROGRAMS; Jail Enforcement Model / Task Force Model / Warrant Service Officer; ICE.gov/287g
description: An informational poster on a dark navy textured (denim/canvas-like) background. The header reads 'ICE local and state law enforcement' in smaller white text above a large white serif title 'PARTNER PROGRAMS'. Down the left side are three stacked photo tiles tinted in red and blue duotone: the top tile (red) shows a jail/cell-block scene; the middle tile (blue) shows officers in tactical gear with 'POLICE' visible; the bottom tile (grayscale) shows a hand writing/signing a document. To the right of each tile is a bold white heading with description: 'Jail Enforcement Model: ID and process removable aliens you arrest with active or pending criminal charges.'; 'Task Force Model: Allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority in certain situations.'; 'Warrant Service Officer: Serve and carry out administrative warrants against illegal aliens.' Bottom left reads 'ICE.gov/287g' and bottom right shows the DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'.

notable_text: This is the highest rate of (worksite enforcement) arrest in HSI's history. - ROBERT HAMMER, ICE HSI Acting Director
description: Quote card with a solid medium-blue background. At top center is a circular cropped headshot of a smiling middle-aged man with a shaved head and gray goatee, wearing a dark suit and a light blue tie, standing before an American flag and a blue flag. Below, framed by large light-blue quotation marks, white serif text reads: 'This is the highest rate of (worksite enforcement) arrest in HSI's history.' The attribution beneath reads 'ROBERT HAMMER' with 'ICE HSI Acting Director' in italics. The bottom right shows the DHS seal and the large white 'ICE' wordmark.

notable_text: Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien: UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 8 to 10 / COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS 2; ICE.gov/detainers

The headline at top in white sans-serif reads 'Number of law enforcement officers needed to arrest a criminal alien:'. Below are two side-by-side columns introduced by colored banner labels: a red banner reading 'UNCOOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the left and a blue banner reading 'COOPERATIVE JURISDICTIONS' on the right, separated by a thin vertical divider. Under the red side is a large white numeral '8 to 10' with text: 'ICE needs more officers to help protect the public from unpredictable behavior by an at-large criminal attempting to evade justice.' Under the blue side is a large white numeral '2' with text: 'ICE can arrest a criminal alien with as few as two officers in a safe, controlled location, like a jail or court lockup area.' The bottom-left bears the DHS/ICE seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement', and the bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/detainers'.

notable_text: CNN's promotion of an 'ICE spotting' app is reckless and irresponsible...My officers and agents are already facing a 500% increase in assaults...CNN is willfully endangering the lives of officers...Is this simply reckless 'journalism' or overt activism?
description: Quote card built around a black-and-white screenshot of a CNN remote video interview, framed by a yellow border. The top half shows a split-screen: on the left a woman seated at a laptop, identified by a red-and-white chyron 'CNN TECH REPORTER / Clare Duffy' with the red 'CNN' logo in the upper corner; on the right a bearded man with glasses speaking from a kitchen. The bottom half is a black panel with white text introduced by a large yellow quotation mark: 'CNN's promotion of an ‘ICE spotting’ app is reckless and irresponsible. Advertising an app that basically paints a target on federal law enforcement officers’ backs is sickening. My officers and agents are already facing a 500% increase in assaults, and going on live television to announce an app that lets anyone zero in on their locations is like inviting violence against them with a national megaphone. CNN is willfully endangering the lives of officers who put their lives on the line every day and enabling dangerous criminal aliens to evade U.S. law.' A closing line in yellow text reads 'Is this simply reckless ‘journalism’ or overt activism?”'

notable_text: MAKING AMERICA SAFE AGAIN; 'Noem threatens to send more immigrants to El Salvador prison'; 'Noem comes face-to-face with alleged Tren de Aragua members'
description: DHS graphic styled as a headline collage on a dark navy-blue textured background that resembles faded newspaper print. A red banner across the top carries bold white all-caps text reading 'MAKING AMERICA SAFE AGAIN'. Below it, five torn-paper or ripped-newsprint cards are arranged in a staggered grid, each reproducing a news outlet logo above a quoted headline about Secretary Kristi Noem's visit to El Salvador's CECOT prison. The cards read: (1) AXIOS - 'Noem warns immigrants during tour of El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans held'; (2) AP - 'Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held'; (3) Washington Examiner - 'Noem sends warning to illegal immigrants from El Salvador prison with Tren de Aragua members'; (4) FOX NEWS - 'Noem comes face-to-face with alleged Tren de Aragua members during tour of notorious El Salvador prison'; (5) The Washington Post - 'Noem threatens to send more immigrants to El Salvador prison'. The circular DHS seal is centered at the bottom.
